intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer gradual, animated state changes over instant refreshes when tracking health data
facade.label: Smooth slide-in updates
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard data reveals itself through fluid motion; metrics fade in, charts animate on load, stat cards slide from edges
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard prefers instant, no-motion updates; all data appears fully formed the moment the screen loads

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer gradual, animated state changes over instant refreshes when tracking health data
evidence.implication: Dashboard data reveals itself through fluid motion; metrics fade in, charts animate on load, stat cards slide from edges

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer conversational, encouraging tone over clinical language when tracking health experiments
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and Personality — friendly vs professional
facade.label: Friendly checklists
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use warm, supportive language; celebrate progress; feel like a health buddy rather than a medical chart
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should prioritize clean, clinical, objective tone; minimal personality; focus on data clarity over encouragement

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard feels more approachable and less clinical with gentle, curved edges rather than sharp corners
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers a friendly, welcoming aesthetic that feels less like medical software and more like a personal companion app
facade.rejectImplies: User gravitates toward crisp, modern, minimal design with defined edges—possibly preferring clarity and precision over warmth

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer organic, approachable UI over clinical precision for health tracking—softness signals safety and care rather than sterility
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use gentle curves, avoid sharp edges; suggests preference for warmth and accessibility in health interface design
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer clean, minimalist, or technical aesthetic; sharper lines and higher contrast could feel more trustworthy for data

facade.hypothesis: Users building health dashboards want prominent key metrics displayed as large typography alongside minimal visualizations
facade.axisTargeted: charts vs text — do users prioritize large readable numbers or at-a-glance visual patterns?
facade.label: Big numbers, small charts
facade.acceptImplies: User wants dashboard hierarchy weighted toward numerical readouts; charts are secondary. Suggests preference for scannable metrics over visual analysis.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers visual-first or balanced layouts. May want charts as primary entry point, or dense data views. Suggests charts vs text axis leans toward visual or equal weighting.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking experience
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking experience
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a persistent left sidebar for experiment navigation paired with card-based layouts for individual health data, creating a stable entry point while keeping content scannable
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values persistent navigation anchors and modular content organization; future probes should test sidebar width, card density, and whether cards should stack vertically or horizontally
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer top navigation, tabs, or list-based layouts instead; next probes should test tabbed interfaces or full-width scrolling lists as alternatives

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer discrete, attention-grabbing metric updates over continuous real-time streaming when tracking health data
facade.axisTargeted: MOTION AND BEHAVIOR — animated vs static, transitions vs instant, gesture vs click, fluid vs snappy
facade.label: Numbers pop in
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard metrics should animate with snappy entrance effects (scale, bounce, or flash) to signal new data and draw focus to what changed
facade.rejectImplies: Smooth, gradual animations work better than punchy discrete updates; users find sudden metric changes jarring or distracting in a health context
